description: The Hague Summit Declaration, adopted unanimously by all 32 NATO member states (Spain received a partial exemption), commits allies to invest 5% of GDP annually on total defense requirements and defense-and-security-related spending by 2035. This is a second, higher-order commitment beyond the 3.5% GDP 'core defense' floor by 2028 (registered as cat_20260603_nato_defense_gdp). Distinction: the 3.5% target covers direct military capability spending by 2028; the 5% target includes the broader defense-and-security envelope (civil resilience, critical infrastructure protection, cyber defense) by 2035. Context: Norway has surpassed the US in defense spending per capita — the first European ally to do so in NATO history. Combined NATO defense spending exceeded $1.5 trillion in 2026 for the first time ever. Poland spent 4.48% of GDP on defense in 2025. Trump announced 5,000 additional US troops to Poland (May 22, 2026), signaling continued US commitment. The 5% by 2035 commitment creates a multi-decade spending floor that extends and reinforces the struct_nato_rearmament structural catalyst.
user_notes: Segunda escalada del marco de gasto NATO en el mismo día. cat_20260603_nato_defense_gdp cubrió 3.5% por 2028 (core defense). Este evento cubre 5% por 2035 (total defense + security). Son compromisos complementarios del mismo Hague Summit, no duplicados. El 25% priced_in refleja que la dirección era esperada pero el nivel 5% + unanimidad + Spain partial exemption tienen elementos de sorpresa. Spain partial exemption: no afecta la thesis para el inversor español — los ETFs (EUDF.L, DFEN.DE) tienen Rheinmetall (DEU), Leonardo (ITA), Airbus Defence (EU), no empresas españolas. relation_to_structural = confirms → usará confirmation_amplifier (×1.12) en el heatmap scoring para eu_defense_prime_contractors.
